candidate_problems.text: Develop a concrete metric for 'mutual constraint' or 'consequence density' that separates genuinely load-bearing explanations from rhetorically tight but false ones.

Test whether problem-solution isomorphism is necessary for explanatory hardness-to-vary in domains where successful explanations are highly abstract or statistical rather than structurally mirroring the phenomenon.
Determine how much linguistic reformulation an explanation can tolerate before apparent counterfactual fragility is revealed to be presentation-dependent rather than structural.
